name: ML Metrics
description: How model performance is measured for classification and regression.

group.definition: A table comparing predicted and actual classes, built from **True Positive**, **False Positive**, **False Negative** and **True Negative** counts.
A positive case that the model correctly predicted as positive.
A negative case the model incorrectly predicted as positive — a 'false alarm' that lowers **Precision**.
A positive case the model incorrectly predicted as negative — a 'miss' that lowers **Recall**.
A negative case that the model correctly predicted as negative.
The fraction of all predictions that are correct; can be misleading on imbalanced datasets, where **Precision** and **Recall** matter more.
Of all cases predicted positive, the fraction that are truly positive — hurt by every **False Positive**.
Of all actual positive cases, the fraction the model correctly finds — hurt by every **False Negative**.
The harmonic mean of **Precision** and **Recall**, balancing the two into a single number.
A plot of the true positive rate against the false positive rate across all classification thresholds.
The area under the **ROC Curve**; 1.0 is a perfect classifier and 0.5 is no better than random guessing.
A metric that heavily penalizes confident but incorrect probability predictions.
Mean Absolute Error — the average absolute difference between predicted and actual values.
Root Mean Squared Error — like **MAE**, but it squares errors first, so large mistakes are penalized far more heavily.
